title: campanula rapunculus var. elatior
intro: Campanula rapunculus var. elatior, also known as the tall rampion, is a biennial plant that belongs to the Campanulaceae family and is native to Europe. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Campanula rapunculus var. elatior include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
